answer:
The correct answer is C. After six years of root denudation, the exposed root surface would have been colonized by oral epithelium, so a lateral sliding flap would show epithelium adjacent to that previously denuded area. New connective tissue attachment (PDL fibers) to cementum or dentin is unlikely without guided tissue regeneration. Thus, the most accurate finding is epithelial contact with the root surface.

\boxed{C}
